content.full_text:
JIM CUNNEEN                                for Assembly '92

FPPC
Mar 25 8 50 AM '92

March 24, 1992

VIA FEDERAL EXPRESS

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:Jim Cunneen for Assembly '92, Committee 911275

I am requesting confirmation of advice I received from Alice Hughes of your office on March 24, 1992 over the telephone.

BACK GROUND

Mr. Cunneen withdrew from the 1992 election for the 22nd Assembly District in December of 1991 and has not raised any funds or made any expenditure toward this election during 1992. In January of 1992 the committee did make a contribution of $1,000 to another candidate, return $350 in contributions received during 1991 and pay approximately $250 in accrued expenses.

Base upon previous advice received in your letter dated February 6, 1992 (File No. A-92-27) Mr. Cunneen has decided to amend the committee to reflect a future election, terminate the current 501 and redesignate the bank account for the future election by amending form 502. In any event Mr. Cunneen will not appear on the ballot during 1992.

Mr. Cunneen is currently on staff for a member of the U.S. House of Representatives and as such has requested a ruling from the House Ethics Committee regarding this matter. While we expect a ruling soon, the Committee has not responded to our inquiry and as such we have not made the above mentioned filings as of this time.

101 First Street    Number 530    Los Altos, California    94022    415-949-4271

Paid for by the Cunneen for Assembly '92 Committee. Contributions to the Cunneen for Assembly Committee are not deductible as charitable contributions for income tax purposes.
Fred Hower, Treasurer                                                                                                    ♻ recycled paper

Fair Political Practices Commission
March 24, 1992
Page 2

QUESTION

Does Mr. Cunneen need to file pre-election statements with the FPPC during 1992?

ADVICE

No. As long as Mr. Cunneen's name will not appear on the ballot during 1992, a pre-election statement does not need to be filed. Mr. Cunneen does however need to file the regular semi-annual statements.

I would appreciate your confirmation of this advice as soon as possible. I am currently relying on this advice and as such will not file a pre-election campaign statement for the period January 1, 1992 though March 17, 1992.


Very truly yours,

[signature]

Frederick Hower
Treasurer


c: James F. Cunneen
